rationale: The main alert (A0) shows the creation of a WMI CommandLineEventConsumer with a highly obfuscated PowerShell command. Evidence EV-001 and EV-002 show the creation of a WMI event filter and its binding to the consumer, establishing persistence. The decoded command (DER-001) reveals a compressed, base64-encoded PowerShell payload, which is a common technique for malicious activity. The subsequent modification of the filter (EV-003) and binding (EV-004) indicates an attempt to alter or update the persistent mechanism, further supporting malicious intent.
